stem: What is the smallest non-negative integer \( x \) such that \( x \equiv [[r1]] \pmod{[[m1]]} \) and \( x \equiv [[r2]] \pmod{[[m2]]} \)?
solution: Write \( x = [[r1]] + [[m1]]t \) and solve \( [[m1]]t \equiv [[r2]]-[[r1]] \pmod{[[m2]]} \) for the smallest \( t\ge0 \) using the inverse of \( [[m1]] \) mod \( [[m2]] \); this gives \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This just adds the two remainders, ignoring the moduli entirely.
This subtracts the remainders from the product of the moduli for no valid reason.
This swaps which modulus's inverse is used, mixing up the roles of the two congruences.
This swaps the two remainders, solving the system with \( r_1 \) and \( r_2 \) exchanged.
This is one more than the correct smallest solution.
This is one less than the correct smallest solution.

stem: What is the smallest non-negative integer \( x \) such that \( x \equiv [[r1]] \pmod{[[m1]]} \), \( x \equiv [[r2]] \pmod{[[m2]]} \), and \( x \equiv [[r3]] \pmod{[[m3]]} \)?
solution: First combine the first two congruences into a single one modulo \( [[m1]]\cdot[[m2]]=[[m12]] \), giving \( x\equiv[[x12]]\pmod{[[m12]]} \); then combine that with the third congruence to get \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This only combines the first two congruences and stops before folding in the third.
This just adds the three remainders, ignoring the moduli entirely.
This subtracts the correct answer from the product of all three moduli for no valid reason.
This subtracts the two-modulus combined modulus from the final answer, landing on the wrong residue.
This is one more than the correct smallest solution.
This is one less than the correct smallest solution.

stem: The smallest non-negative integer \( x \) satisfying \( x \equiv [[r1]] \pmod{[[m1]]} \) and \( x \equiv [[r2]] \pmod{[[m2]]} \) is \( [[x0]] \). What is the SECOND smallest positive integer satisfying both congruences?
solution: Since \( [[m1]] \) and \( [[m2]] \) are coprime, all solutions repeat with period \( \mathrm{lcm}([[m1]],[[m2]]) = [[m1]]\cdot[[m2]] \); the next solution after \( [[x0]] \) is \( [[x0]] + [[m1]]\cdot[[m2]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the two moduli instead of their product (the true period, since they're coprime).
This doubles the first solution instead of adding the period.
This advances by only one modulus, which does not preserve the OTHER congruence.
This advances by only the other modulus, which does not preserve the FIRST congruence.
This is one more than the correct second-smallest solution.
This is one less than the correct second-smallest solution.
